paper_metadata.title: Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models
paper_metadata.abstract: Language models have achieved remarkable performance on a wide range of tasks that require natural language understanding. Nevertheless, state-of-the-art models have generally struggled with tasks that require quantitative reasoning, such as solving mathematics, science, and engineering problems at the college level. To help close this gap, we introduce Minerva, a large language model pretrained on general natural language data and further trained on technical content. The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on technical benchmarks without the use of external tools. We also evaluate our model on over two hundred undergraduate-level problems in physics, biology, chemistry, economics, and other sciences that require quantitative reasoning, and find that the model can correctly answer nearly a third of them.

extraction_data.innovation: Minerva's key innovation is a large-scale training dataset that juxtaposes natural language with formal mathematical language (equations and diagrams) to enable robust quantitative reasoning without external tools.
extraction_data.research_problem: Quantitative reasoning in mathematics, science, and engineering
extraction_data.application: Solving quantitative reasoning problems in mathematics, science, and engineering (e.g., physics, biology, chemistry, economics, engineering)
extraction_data.paper_title: Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models

extraction_data.innovation: The paper demonstrates that larger language models (540B) can solve quantitative reasoning problems more accurately than smaller ones (62B), with the 540B model correcting errors made by the 62B model in complex mathematical reasoning tasks.
